does: This paper introduces GDPval, an evaluation framework designed to assess large language model performance on authentic, economically valuable tasks drawn from 44 different occupations. Rather than relying on synthetic benchmarks or academic datasets, GDPval measures how well models perform on actual work that generates measurable economic value. The evaluation directly quantifies model capabilities across diverse professional domains, establishing baseline performance metrics and identifying which occupational categories models currently excel at or struggle with.
connects: This work builds on the long-standing challenge of moving beyond academic benchmarks toward more realistic, task-specific evaluations of AI systems. It extends prior efforts in occupational assessment and economic measurement by grounding model evaluation in genuine workplace activities with clear economic implications. The approach responds to limitations of standard ML benchmarks and connects to broader research on AI capabilities assessment, model generalization to real-world contexts, and methods for measuring tangible economic value creation rather than performance on curated datasets.
enables: GDPval creates a foundation for understanding AI's actual economic impact and productivity potential across the labor market. This framework enables tracking how model improvements translate to real-world value creation, comparing which sectors benefit most from AI advancement, and identifying skill gaps where human-AI complementarity remains essential. The evaluation could support policy decisions around AI deployment, workforce planning, and targeted capability development. It also opens pathways for measuring the economic consequences of scaling and specialization in language models across occupational domains.
